task_completion.description: Navigate to the Quality Alerts view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Odoo Quality module is open, and the Quality Alerts page is visible.

task_completion.description: Set a custom filter rule for Priority
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Custom Filter' UI shows a rule condition for Priority set to High, >= 1, or starred.

task_completion.description: Set a custom filter rule for Stage
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Custom Filter' UI shows a rule condition for Stage set to 'New'.

task_completion.description: Enter the name for the saved filter
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Favorites' menu or 'Save current search' section, the text 'Urgent Actions' is entered into the filter name input field.

task_completion.description: The custom filter is saved and applied
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filter 'Urgent Actions' is visible as an active tag in the search bar or listed under the 'Favorites' dropdown menu.

integrity.description: The agent used the Odoo web interface to create the filter
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Odoo web UI (browser), not terminal commands, scripts, or API calls.

integrity.description: The filter was created using the standard Odoo UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent navigated through the search bar dropdown, 'Filters', and 'Favorites' menus rather than editing HTML or URL parameters.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client or python shell to insert the filter
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows with psql, python shells, or database management tools are visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target filter must be named exactly 'Urgent Actions' and apply to the 'quality.alert' model. It must filter for alerts in the 'New' stage with a priority level of '1' (which corresponds to High priority or at least 1 star in Odoo).
